accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text and accurate context; the Energy and Commerce committee referral and procedural history are correct.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; assigning functions to an Assistant Secretary reflects the amended section 203(a) which lists Assistant Secretary functions.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill's purpose to assign energy emergency and security functions within DOE.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly states the bill adds a new paragraph to Section 203(a) of the Department of Energy Organization Act.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes the technical assistance is conditioned upon request from State, local, Tribal, or energy sector entities and coordinated with federal agencies.

effective_dates.why: The bill specifies no effective dates or deadlines, and the summary correctly notes their absence.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures both the new functions paragraph and the coordination directive to the Secretary.

objective_tone.why: Neutral, descriptive language with no editorializing.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names the Department of Energy, the Secretary, the Act, and the Committee on Energy and Commerce.

correct_figures.why: The introduction date of January 27, 2026 and section reference are accurate.
